# Meshvise

> French industrial supervision (SCADA) platform: flat annual licence, customer-hosted, data in standard PostgreSQL queryable from Power BI, Grafana or Excel. French company, French law, GDPR by design, no Cloud Act exposure.

Meshvise acquires data from industrial PLCs over Modbus TCP, OPC-UA and BACnet/IP, exposes it live (sub-100 ms WebSocket push) and archives it into a TimescaleDB hypertable. Built for small-to-mid industrial and commercial sites (50 to 500 measurement points) where legacy tag-priced SCADA platforms are too expensive and SaaS-only platforms are unacceptable for data residency.

## What Meshvise is

- A web-based SCADA / IIoT supervision platform.
- Self-hosted via Docker Compose (single command). Docker deployment in 4 hours, first point read in 30 minutes.
- Backend: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + asyncpg + SQLAlchemy 2 (async).
- Database: PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB.
- Frontend: Vue 3 + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Pinia (single-page app).
- Real-time: Redis pub/sub forwarded to browser via WebSocket.

## What Meshvise is not

- Not a safety / SIL-rated control system. Use a certified PLC for interlocks. Meshvise only supervises, alarms, archives.
- Not (yet) a wiresheet visual logic editor. The execution engine ships, the visual editor lands Q3 2026.
- Not a full HMI builder. No drag-drop screen designer (planned later).

## Capabilities shipped today

- Multi-protocol acquisition: Modbus TCP, OPC-UA, BACnet/IP.
- Live points (read + write) over WebSocket, polling fallback.
- TimescaleDB historian with per-point on_change deadband or fixed interval.
- Operational alarms: high/low thresholds with deadband and delay, severities (info/warning/critical), notification email groups, full event log.
- Tag-based organisation: equipment is tagged once (site, building, function, criticality), views are filtered or composed from those tags. Adding new equipment with the right tags makes it appear automatically in every relevant view.
- Git-style config versioning: every change goes into a revision. Diff between two revisions, single-transaction revert, audit trail of who changed what when. Hot-reload via pg_notify.
- Direct data access: REST API on /py/v1/{drivers,machines,points,alarms,revisions} and WebSocket /ws/live. Native ODBC PostgreSQL connection: Power BI, Grafana, Metabase, Excel connect directly to the database.
- Multi-project / multi-tenant: project_id scoping on every entity.

## Sovereignty and compliance

- Hosted by the customer, on customer infrastructure. No telemetry, no phone-home, no customer data on Meshvise infrastructure. Air-gap compatible for sites disconnected from the internet.
- GDPR compliant by design: no personal data leaves the customer instance by default. DPA provided with the Standard contract.
- French law, French-language contract, jurisdiction in Paris. No offshore arbitration clause.
- No US Cloud Act, no extra-territorial reach, no foreign cloud-vendor dependency on the production path.

## Differentiators vs legacy SCADA platforms

- Flat-fee licence: Standard tier is €990/year per site for unlimited points, machines, drivers, users, and features. Tag-priced suites typically scale linearly with site size and add per-protocol modules.
- Data in standard PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB. No proprietary historian format. Power BI, Grafana, Metabase, Excel connect via standard ODBC. No export step, no middleware.
- Self-hosted by default. Customer data never leaves the customer network. Runs on a Raspberry Pi 4, an industrial mini-PC, or a 5 EUR/month VPS. No JVM, no station application.
- Versioned configuration with one-click revert. Industrial supervision platforms traditionally rely on manual backup. Meshvise commits changes like a git repository and lets the customer roll back to any deployed revision in a single transaction.

## Hardware footprint

- Minimum: 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB disk.
- Tested on: Hetzner CX22, Raspberry Pi 4, industrial mini-PCs.
- Demo runs on a 4 EUR/month nano VPS.

## Pricing (three doors, one paid tier per ADR-0008)

- Online demo: 0 EUR, public, on app.meshvise.com. No install, no sign-up. Simulated industrial-site dataset (10 drivers, 13 machines, 18 alarms). 60 seconds to click around.
- 7-day trial: 0 EUR, via the meshvise.com/<lang>/trial/ form. Full app on the customer infrastructure, limits 5 drivers / 15 machines / 500 points, all features. After 7 days the app switches to read-only, customer data and configuration stay accessible.
- Standard: 990 EUR/year per site (excl. VAT). Unlimited points, machines, drivers, users, features. All protocols (Modbus TCP + OPC-UA + BACnet/IP). Updates included. Email support at contact@meshvise.com with a 1-business-day SLA (French public holidays excluded). Source-code audit under NDA on request. One instance per licence.

There is no Discovery permanent self-host tier. The online demo plays that role for evaluation; the trial plays it for in-conditions validation.

## How to evaluate

Three doors depending on the prospect's need:

- Online demo: https://app.meshvise.com (no signup, public, simulated industrial-site data). 60 seconds to click around and see the UI.
- 7-day trial on the customer's infrastructure: https://meshvise.com/fr/trial/ (or https://meshvise.com/en/trial/). Fill the form, receive a license.jwt + docker-compose.yml by email, run `docker compose up -d` on the customer's server, wire real PLCs, validate in real conditions.
- Standard production: contact contact@meshvise.com for a quote and a permanent licence tied to the customer's project_id.

## Distribution and licensing

Closed-source commercial product. The full backend ships as a compiled Docker image on ghcr.io. Licence enforcement uses Ed25519-signed JWT licences (offline verification, no phone-home, air-gap-safe). Customer data lives entirely on the customer's PostgreSQL on the customer's server. The standard SQL data format means there is no vendor lock-in even though the platform itself is closed source.

Source-code audit available under NDA for Standard customers, on request, for compliance needs (security audit, regulatory validation, due diligence).

## Project status

Production-ready as of Q2 2026. Wiresheet visual editor targeted for Q3 2026. Distant multi-site supervision with edge gateways and a consolidating central instance targeted for Q4 2026.
